lede: A look at November 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Charles de Gaulle and Yaroslav Rybakov, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Max Headroom signal hijackings
events.desc: The Max Headroom signal hijacking incident takes place, in which a pirate broadcast interrupts television broadcasts in Chicago.

events.title: Cairngorm Plateau disaster
events.desc: In Britain's worst mountaineering tragedy, the Cairngorm Plateau Disaster, five children and one of their leaders are found dead from exposure in the Scottish mountains.

events.title: John F. Kennedy
events.desc: U.S. President John F. Kennedy is assassinated and Texas Governor John Connally is seriously wounded by Lee Harvey Oswald, who also kills Dallas Police officer J. D. Tippit after fleeing the scene. Vice President Lyndon B. Johnson is sworn in as the 36th President of the United States afterwards.

events.title: Lebanon
events.desc: Lebanon gains independence from France, nearly two years after it was first announced by the Free French government.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Following the initial Italian invasion, Greek troops counterattack into Italian-occupied Albania and capture Korytsa.

events.title: SS Ville du Havre
events.desc: The French steamer SS Ville du Havre sinks in 12 minutes after colliding with the Scottish iron clipper Loch Earn in the Atlantic, with a loss of 226 lives.

events.title: Birmingham
events.desc: In Birmingham, England, Albert, Prince Consort lays the foundation stone of the Birmingham and Midland Institute.

events.title: Dutch Formosa
events.desc: Dutch colonial forces on Taiwan launch a pacification campaign against native villages, resulting in Dutch control of the middle and south of the island.

events.title: Juan Fernández (explorer)
events.desc: Spanish navigator Juan Fernández discovers islands now known as the Juan Fernández Islands off Chile.

events.title: Brittany
events.desc: The first duke of Brittany, Nominoe, defeats the Frankish king Charles the Bald at the Battle of Ballon near Redon.

births.name: Charles de Gaulle
births.line: French general and politician, President of France

births.name: Terry Gilliam
births.line: American-English actor, director, animator, and screenwriter

births.name: André Gide
births.line: French novelist, essayist, and dramatist, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: John F. Kennedy, American politician, 35th President of the United States
C. S. Lewis, British writer, literary scholar, and Anglican lay theologian

observances.text: Good Spouses Day (いい夫婦の日) celebrates married couples in Japan.
Independence Day celebrates the independence of Lebanon from France in 1943.
